title: Skywalker Tour
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Quote|'''Relive the adventures''' of one of the Rebellion's most famous heroes—take the SKYWALKER TOUR! Journey by sail barge as your guide points out the historic places where Luke Skywalker began his rendezvous with history!|[[Tatooine Visitors Bureau]] brochure|<ref name="Star Wars Technical Journal">''[[Star Wars Technical Journal]]''</ref>}}
The '''Skywalker Tour''' was a popular tour on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] that visited locations that were host to events that were considered crucial to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] decision to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], [[Myhr Rho]], an enterprising [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]], began a daily tour to the locations listed below. The Skywalker Tour became much more successful as word of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] defeat at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] spread, and the tour was able to upgrade the mode of transportation to that of a [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]]. However, evidence suggests that at some point between [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] the tours either stopped visiting certain locations or ended altogether.

==The Tour==
This is a list of the places that the Skywalker Tour made stops at and the order in which they were visited by the tour.
*1) Landing site of [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] from [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] carrying [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. After much bickering, the droids parted ways.
*2) Site of the capture of R2-D2 by [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]].
*3) Site of the capture of C-3PO by Jawas. Stops 2 and 3 were not in chronological order historically. Glad that he had been saved and unaware of the fate of droids that had been "rescued" by Jawas, C-3PO was happy to inform the Jawas of the last known location and direction of his wayward counterpart.
*4) The [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]] and [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Lars moisture farm remained abandoned until Luke Skywalker sold the farm to a [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Throgg]], who then sold it to the [[Darklighter family/Legends|Darklighter family]]. Consultation of notes on [[vidmap]]s of the area indicated that the Darklighters were happy to have some company. However it was unknown whether they tolerated groups of tourists that had come by to see where Luke Skywalker had slept, or where [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen]] and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]] had been murdered.
*5) Place where Luke Skywalker found R2-D2, was ambushed by [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand people]], and was rescued by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].
*6) [[Ben Kenobi's home/Legends|Ben Kenobi's hut]]. This building was restored and preserved for the Skywalker Tour. However, as of approximately [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], it was discovered that it had been ransacked and had become a home for various [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] creatures.
*7) Site of destroyed Jawa [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]]. In spite of the fact that the sandcrawler had essentially disappeared due to the scavenging activities of other Jawas, this site remained historically significant in the context of Skywalker's decision to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]].
*8) [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. Records don't indicate exactly which sites in Mos Eisley were visited by the Skywalker Tour. It is likely that stops within Mos Eisley would have included:
:*The [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley Cantina]]
:*[[Spaceport Speeders/Legends|Spaceport Speeders]]
:*[[Docking Bay 94/Legends|Docking Bay 94]]

===Jabba's Palace and The Great Pit of Carkoon===
In addition to the places listed above, the Skywalker Tour visited [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|former palace]] and the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]]. Not much is known about the renovations to the palace, other than the fact that it boasted an accurate replica of the infamous piece of art ''[[Man in Carbonite]]''. The difficulty with which it took Luke Skywalker and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to enter the former palace in the year [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] suggests that the [[B'omarr Order/Legends|B'omarr Monks]] who inhabited it disliked the tourists and sealed off all of the entrances.

The stop at the Great Pit of Carkoon featured the gutted remains of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] sail barge, robots depicting Jabba and his minions, and the [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]]. It is likely that some cautionary measures were taken so as to prevent tourists from tumbling down the pit.

==Appearances==
*''[[Tatooine Ghost]]''
*[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]
